name: fastapi-router-py
description: Create FastAPI routers with CRUD operations, authentication dependencies, and proper response models. Use when building REST API endpoints, creating new routes, implementing CRUD operations, or adding authenticated endpoints in FastAPI applications.
capabilities: Generate FastAPI router files with CRUD endpoints
Add optional or required authentication dependencies
Configure response models for type-safe responses
Set appropriate HTTP status codes for each operation
Create PascalCase and snake_case placeholder replacements
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 17 patterns (external_commands and weak cryptographic algorithm), but all are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation template containing Python code examples in fenced blocks, not executable code. No actual shell execution or cryptographic operations exist.
valueStatement: Building REST APIs requires consistent router patterns with authentication and proper responses. This skill generates production-ready FastAPI routers following established conventions.
userTitle: Generate FastAPI Router Templates with Authentication
limitations: Only generates router templates, not database models or service layers
Requires manual integration with existing authentication systems
Does not generate API documentation or OpenAPI schemas
Template-based output may need customization for complex use cases

useCases.title: Rapid API Endpoint Creation
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold new resource endpoints with standard CRUD operations and authentication.

useCases.title: Consistent Router Patterns
useCases.description: Ensure all team members follow the same router structure with proper response models and status codes.

useCases.title: Learning FastAPI Best Practices
useCases.description: Study generated templates to understand authentication dependencies and response model patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Router Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a FastAPI router for a 'Product' resource with standard CRUD endpoints. Use 'product' as the snake_case name and 'products' as the plural form.

promptTemplates.title: Router with Required Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a FastAPI router for 'Task' management that requires authentication for all endpoints. Include proper 401 handling for unauthorized requests.

promptTemplates.title: Router with Optional Authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a FastAPI router for 'Article' where listing is public but create, update, and delete require authentication. Show both dependency patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Full Integration Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete router for 'Comment' resource with: (1) router file in src/backend/app/routers/, (2) mounting code for main.py, (3) Pydantic models for request/response, (4) service layer stub.

bestPractices: Always define response_model to ensure type safety and filter sensitive fields
Use appropriate HTTP status codes: 201 for creation, 204 for deletion, 200 for reads
Separate optional and required authentication dependencies clearly in function signatures
antiPatterns: Avoid mixing optional and required auth in the same endpoint - use separate endpoints
Do not skip response_model on endpoints returning sensitive user data
Never return raw database models directly without Pydantic serialization

faq.question: What is the difference between get_current_user and get_current_user_required?
faq.answer: get_current_user returns Optional[User] and allows anonymous access, while get_current_user_required raises 401 if not authenticated and always returns a User object.

faq.question: Do I need to create Pydantic models separately?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill generates the router structure. You must create corresponding Pydantic models for request bodies and response schemas in your models directory.

faq.question: How do I mount the generated router in my application?
faq.answer: Import the router in main.py and use app.include_router(router, prefix='/api/resource'). The skill provides the mounting code snippet.

faq.question: Can I customize the HTTP status codes for specific operations?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated template uses standard codes (201 for creation, 204 for deletion) but you can modify the status_code parameter on any route decorator.

faq.question: Does this skill handle database operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill only generates the router layer. Database operations should be implemented in a separate service layer that the router calls.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with FastAPI version 0.100+?
faq.answer: Yes, the generated code uses modern Python type hints and FastAPI patterns compatible with version 0.100 and above, including the list[Item] response syntax.
